steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A linear function with slope $m$ and $y$-intercept $b$ can be written as
$$f(x)=mx+b$$

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content: The slope is $$\frac{1}{4}$$, and the point $(0,3)$ gives a $y$-intercept of $3$. $$f(x)=\frac{1}{4}x+3$$ The answer is A.
